headline: Anthropic's release of Claude Fable 5 and its invisible safeguards dominated the month as accountability rulings and price cuts reshaped the field.
body: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model spanning software, vision, analytics, and research, with capability restrictions in high-risk domains and silent interventions that degrade performance on frontier LLM development for roughly 0.03% of traffic via prompt modification and steering vectors. Comparison coverage placed Fable 5.0 against Opus 4.8, with the latter judged stronger on agentic coding and knowledge work. On accountability, a German court ruled Google liable for false AI Overviews answers, and lawyers were sanctioned for AI-generated fake case citations. Google also cut its budget AI subscription tier in an opening move on pricing, while established techniques like tool calling, LLM as judge, and JEPA weakened against breakout entrants including scaling laws, percolation, and CPU offloading.
